title: Logical error: 'Unexpected return type from coalesce. Expected UInt32. Got UInt16
description:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t1 (x UInt8) ENGINE = Memory;
INSERT INTO t1 VALUES (1);
CREATE TABLE t2 (x UInt16) ENGINE = Memory;
INSERT INTO t2 VALUES (1);
CREATE TABLE t3 (x UInt32) ENGINE = Memory;
INSERT INTO t3 VALUES (1);
SET query_plan_use_new_logical_join_step = 0;
SELECT coalesce(t2.x, t1.x) FROM t1 FULL OUTER JOIN t2 USING (x) FULL OUTER JOIN t3 USING (x)
```
```
[avogar-dev] 2025.11.03 15:35:50.973172 [ 1077928 ] {1bdc7036-ea62-4ce5-ab9c-3257e4a7e7a8} <Fatal> : Logical error: 'Unexpected return type from coalesce. Expected UInt32. Got UInt16. Action:
FUNCTION coalesce(__table2.x :: 1, __table1.x :: 0) -> coalesce(__table2.x, __table1.x) UInt32 : 2,
input block structure:__table2.x UInt16 UInt16(size = 1), __table1.x UInt32 UInt32(size = 1)'.

17. start_thread @ 0x0000000000094ac3
18. __clone3 @ 0x00000000001268c0
```
Seems like the problem is that in joined tables we have columns with different types - `UInt8`, `UInt16` and `UInt32`. And for the JOIN result we use least common type - `UInt32` for this column. And we decide that the result type of `coalesce` should be also `UInt32`. But then actual columns passed to `coalesce` seems to have types `UInt8` and `UInt16` and we call `multiIf` inside `coalesce` with these columns and get `UInt16` result.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t4 (x INTEGER, y INTEGER NULL);
CREATE TABLE t5 (x INTEGER, y INTEGER);
SELECT COALESCE(t4.y, t5.y) FROM t4 FULL OUTER JOIN t5 ON t4.x = t5.x;
key_bug_features: Coalesce function with mixed column types in JOIN
